scope: The runner treats BLOCKED as terminal-for-the-lane: a dispatched implementer returning an explicit blocked signal, or a verifier BLOCKED verdict, ends all dispatches for that slice in the run, halts the owning sequential track, consumes no retry budget, and exits non-zero with a report distinguishing BLOCKED lanes (blocker verbatim, routed to /replan-release) from FAIL lanes (retries exhausted). FAIL keeps its existing retryable semantics unchanged.

PASS: TestLoopBlockedImplementerTerminal (AC-01), TestLoopBlockedVerifierTerminal (AC-02), TestLoopFailRetrySemanticsUnchanged (AC-03), TestLoopExitReportBlockedVsFail (AC-05) in internal/run; TestLoopBlockedSliceHaltsTrack (AC-04) in internal/scheduler

reachability.evidence: go test -count=1 -run TestLoopBlockedImplementerTerminal -v ./internal/run/ — PASS. The fired S05 replay with synthetic verdict objects, driving the REAL run.RunSlice engine entry point (the function sworn loop dispatches) against a real temp git repo: a fake driver's implementer leg returns {StatusBlocked, BlockedReason}; asserts exactly ONE implementer dispatch and ZERO verifier dispatches despite a second escalation model with retries remaining, IsBlocked(err) with the blocker VERBATIM plus the /replan-release directive in the error, and the WRITTEN status.json read back off disk carrying verification.result=blocked, routing=needs_planner, state still in_progress (never certified). Second entry point: go test -count=1 -run TestLoopExitReportBlockedVsFail -v ./internal/run/ — PASS, driving the REAL run.RunParallel (the sworn loop --parallel engine) with one blocked-terminal track and one plain-fail track, asserting the non-nil (exit-1) error renders the BLOCKED section (verbatim blocker, '-> /replan-release test-report') distinct from the FAIL section and that the same report lands in the durable loop log. Dark-code declaration (spec out_of_scope 4, Captain flag (b)): no production driver emits implement-leg StatusBlocked yet — live reachability today is the verify-leg BLOCKED verdict and the S07/S09 terminal auth/credits errors, which now render as BLOCKED lanes as designed (Coach ack pin 3, no reason-string sniffing). Spec-sanctioned, not dark code.

delivered.item: AC-01: implementer blocked=true ends the slice's dispatches for the run, halts the lane, and emits the blocker verbatim with an explicit route-to-/replan-release directive — driver.Result.BlockedReason (additive, zero-value default), implement.Run early return on StatusBlocked (no proof, no implemented transition — also fixes the latent spurious-certification fall-through), RunSlice implement-leg blocked-terminal branch (status blocked + needs_planner routing + single notify + sentinel error, returned BEFORE any triage so resolveCount/modelIdx are untouched).

AC-02: verifier BLOCKED terminates the lane identically without consuming implementer retry budget and is never mapped onto FAIL — existing orchestrator.Decide(Blocked)->Halt path anchored with a 2-model escalation list and retries remaining: 1 implementer + 1 verifier dispatch, verification.result=blocked, state never failed_verification, IsFailed(err)=false.

AC-03: FAIL with retries remaining keeps existing retry semantics — FAIL->PASS run makes exactly 2 implementer dispatches with the verifier's violations text forwarded verbatim into dispatch 2's payload (S44 feedback), retry consumed, slice verified.

AC-04: a blocked slice halts the owning sequential track — WorkerOptions.RecordBlocked side-channel + blockedLaneTerminal helper at all three RunSliceFn-error sites (router implement/verify, redesign, legacy), checked after the pause sentinels and before breaker fingerprinting; slice 2 never dispatched; RecordBlocked invoked exactly once with the verbatim reason (route suffix trimmed); supervisor row persisted supervisor.StateFailed — never 'done' (Captain pin 1, CRITICAL: supervisor.Release coerces unknown states to StateDone).

AC-05: a run ending with any blocked lane exits non-zero with a report distinguishing BLOCKED lanes (blocker verbatim + '-> /replan-release <release>' directive) from FAIL lanes (retries exhausted); the same report lands in the durable loop log; the legacy 'RunParallel: %d track(s) failed: %s' error stays byte-identical when no lane is blocked (protects TestRunParallel_FailureCascade and unknown format-matching callers).

AC-06: all existing retry-cap behaviour unchanged — full go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./... green; zero existing test files edited (all five new tests live in three NEW files; git diff 79fce54..HEAD contains no pre-existing _test.go).
delivered.evidence: full-suite run in test_results; git diff --name-only 79fce54..HEAD

not_delivered: Baton role-prompt return-contract documentation for the blocked field — out of scope by spec (out_of_scope 2). Why: explicitly non-blocking per the inbound packet's ordering ruling; tracking: owned by the later Baton contract-edge work (fired proposal capture docs/captures/2026-07-10-baton-sworn-edge-contracts-proposal.md, Rec 4); acknowledgement: spec.json itself (Coach-authored second-pass replan 2026-07-10).

Production driver emission of implement-leg StatusBlocked — out of scope by spec (out_of_scope 4). Why: drivers map only what their upstream harnesses expose, and no observed CLI exposes an explicit blocked signal; absent a signal they return retryable shapes, never a fabricated blocked=true; tracking: spec out_of_scope 4 + the dark-code declaration in this bundle's reachability section; acknowledgement: Captain review flag (b) + Coach ack pin 4 catch-all (captain-proceed.md@31c3290).

sworn coverage + sworn llm-check --check ac-satisfaction gates not run — why: neither command exists in this branch's binary and no provider credentials are present in this environment; tracking: AC-to-test coverage traced manually in 'delivered' (one named test per AC) and the fresh-context Verifier (Rule 7) backstops — same posture as review.md flag (e); acknowledgement: Coach ack pin 4 catch-all accepting review.md's flags (captain-proceed.md@31c3290).

Release-wide sworn designfit exits 2 on S11-baton-revendor's pre-existing invalid quadrant 'beast' before evaluating any slice — not this slice's defect (T7 planner artefact, outside S14's touchpoints — track collision rule forbids absorbing it). Why: fixing another track's status.json from this session would be a silent cross-track write; tracking: sworn#90 (filed this session); acknowledgement: this proof bundle + journal entry.
divergence: The RunSlice blocked branch writes the status record BEFORE Stage('.')+Commit so the commit is never empty when the blocked dispatch made no file edits (design.md's commit phrasing assumed partial edits exist; same clean-tree intent).

verification.routing uses the literal "needs_planner" with a vocab-binding comment (verdict.Result.Routing doc / board.BlockedNeedsPlanner) rather than importing internal/board into slice.go for the typed constant — no new import coupling; the value is identical at both bound ends.

Captain flag (a) resolved by choosing the trim option: blockedLaneReason strips orchestrator.BlockedLaneRouteSuffix before recording, so the exit report renders the replan directive once per lane (asserted in TestLoopExitReportBlockedVsFail).

Everything else matches design.md exactly, including the declared touchpoints deliberately left untouched: internal/run/resolve.go, internal/run/parallel_test.go, internal/verify/, cmd/sworn/run.go, cmd/sworn/run_test.go.
